event: The Virgin of the Apocalypse triumphing over the dragon
symbols: woman standing on the dragon — the Woman of Revelation 12 crushing the ancient serpent, Mary's triumph over evil
dragon beneath her feet — Satan defeated, the ancient serpent of Genesis 3:15 whose head is crushed
Archangel Michael with shield — the heavenly warrior who casts the dragon from heaven (Rev 12:7-9)
God the Father above — the divine authority behind the cosmic victory
cherubim and angels — the heavenly host participating in the triumph
Christ Child held by an angel — the male child of Revelation 12:5 caught up to God's throne
description: A complex heavenly composition set against a cloudy sky. At center, the Virgin Mary in a dark blue mantle is held aloft by a large angel while standing upon a dark globe or sphere. Below, a grotesque dragon writhes in defeat. To the left, the Archangel Michael in armor with a shield and cape tramples the dragon. Above, God the Father appears in the clouds as an elderly bearded figure. Several cherubim and angels with flowers scatter throughout the scene. To the right, another angel figure holds a mirror or wreath. The palette is rich with deep blues, reds, and warm golden tones.
theological_description: This painting depicts the Virgin of the Apocalypse, a conflation of two scriptural themes: the Woman clothed with the sun from Revelation 12 and the promise of Genesis 3:15 that the seed of the woman would crush the serpent's head. In Revelation 12:1-6, a great sign appears in heaven — a woman clothed with the sun, with the moon under her feet and a crown of twelve stars, who gives birth to a male child destined to rule all nations. The great red dragon seeks to devour the child, but the child is caught up to God's throne. Then Michael and his angels wage war against the dragon and cast it from heaven (Rev 12:7-9). Catholic theology, particularly in the tradition of the Immaculate Conception, reads this apocalyptic woman as a figure of Mary, the new Eve who is preserved from sin and participates in the defeat of evil. The composition presents the entire economy of salvation in a single image: God the Father who sends, the Virgin who bears, the Son who saves, Michael who executes divine judgment, and the dragon — that ancient serpent who is the devil and Satan (Rev 12:9) — utterly defeated beneath the feet of the woman.

scripture_refs.reason: The Woman clothed with the sun, the dragon, and the male child — the direct source of this composition
Michael and his angels casting the dragon from heaven, depicted in the archangel's combat
The Protoevangelium — the woman's seed crushing the serpent's head, fulfilled in Mary's triumph
Identification of the dragon as the ancient serpent, the devil, Satan — the defeated enemy
The Annunciation to Mary, connecting her role as Theotokos to her apocalyptic glorification
